name: Leroy Kirkland
abstract: Leroy Edward Kirkland (February 10, 1904 or 1906 – April 6, 1988) was an American arranger, bandleader, guitarist and songwriter whose career spanned the eras of big band jazz, R&B, rock and roll and soul.

about_quotes.speaker: Erskine Hawkins
about_quotes.evidence: LeRoy Kirkland, the guitar. He did a lot of writing for me. ... Leroy Kirkland. He used to arrange his tunes by giving us and make this arrangement for me

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: he recommended me to Leroy Kirkland... Leroy Kirkland was living in the same building that I was living in... asked me if I could make a record date... we were doing like rhythm and blues

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: And there was the Leroy Kirkland band. He was the one who wrote the charts and directed the seven-piece that we had for that record.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Chic Webb’s guitarist, his name was Leroy Kirkland. And he was one of Chic Webb’s main arrangers you know, a fine musician.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Leroy Kirkland, because Leroy Kirkland was one of the guys that was always on the scene
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
